title: After Thunderbird installer download from Mozilla website, I cannot access either of my 2 yahoo accounts
creator: derekwt
content:
After downloading and running installer (Thunderbird setup 148.0) from Thunderbird website, Thunderbird opened as usual, showing my three email accounts. The 2 accounts I use for almost everything are both Yahoo accounts (yahoo.co.uk and rocketmail.com). But.. neither of these accounts will work without a yahoo sign-in pop which is pre-filled with an account name (example graphic attachment : thunderbird01red.jpg). 1 example shown but the same thing happens for both yahoo accounts.

The 'Next' button, which I assume will ask for password, does nothing.

I have attempted login via webmail. I enter account name, am asked for password and Yahoo insists on sending me a verification code - to the other yahoo account (the yahoo.co.uk account), which I cannot access! I then attempt to login to the other account to access my pass code and the same thing happens in reverse, telling me that a verification code will be sent to the first account.

Via Thunderbird, which has for years done all this automatically behind the scenes, I can get absolutely nowhere. Via webmail, I am in an 'impossibility loop'. I have cleared cache for each account.

Why won't Thunderbird allow automatic account login, as has been the case since the year dot. These are the 2 accounts around which my entire life is ordered - can somebody please help.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:148.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/148.0;os:Windows 10/11

title: Sort Thunderbird right-click "Move to" and "Copy to" list alphabetically
creator: junk-it
content:
Hello,

Why the hell did you guys decide to change the sort order of the "Move to" and "Copy to" Right-Click menu folders? How annoying! This makes Thunderbird *less* user friendly, not more user friendly. Do you guys even use Thunderbird? I suspect not, or you would have realized what a bad decision this was. I use these right-click menus every day, and now, because you guys decided to sort the order based on last used, I have to HUNT for the folder each time, because it is constantly changing! Before, I would know where that folder was in the list before I even right-clicked. Unless this gets fixed, I will go back to an older version of Thunderbird.

Instead of spending time changing features for the worse, how about doing something useful, like making Thunderbird work correctly with Protonmail?

Thanks!

title: Followup to inquiry below
creator: freedomarchives
content:
i am confused at this point: Double-click toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets to true in Settings/General/Config. editor, restart TB.

The row background and text colours can be set with css (see picture):

/* row bg, text colours for selected messages table view*/ tr[is="thread-row"].selected { 

 color: white !important;  background-color: red !important;

}


Help/Troubleshooting Info, Profile Folder, Open Folder, close TB, create a new folder named chrome, create a new document in chrome with a text editor, name it userChrome.css, Save as type: All files *.*, copy in the above code, change the colours as desired. Double-click toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets to true in Settings/General/Config. editor, restart TB.

title: Can receive but cannot send emails from Thunderbird using an office365 mail account.
creator: tmarini
content:
Hello Thunderbird Community Help!

After reviewing and implementing all the suggestions on the Mozilla/Thunderbird support pages and on the internet, I still cannot send emails from my office365 mail account from Thunderbird. I was able to send/receive email messages until about 4-5 days ago. From several different accounts on my laptop. And from two different laptops: one using Windows 10 and the other 11. Now, both will receive emails to their inboxes but neither will send a message.

I have the various set-up parameters set as recommended for SMTP using Oauth2, see below and the attached photo:

Outgoing (SMTP): Server: smtp.office365.com Port: 587 Security: STARTTLS Authentication: OAuth2 Username: Your full Office 365 email address

I have deleted the stored password hundreds of times. I have deactivated MFA with Microsoft. I have deleted the e-mail profile in question many times, and have re-created it using automated and/or manual configurations. And I have deleted stored passwords.

I keep getting this message:

"Login to server smtp.office365.com with username [my.email.address] failed." With three button choices: "Retry" "Enter New Password" "Cancel"

No luck with Retry or Enter New Password. They both result in a brief, failed re-attempt to send the email message, and a return to the previous message and choices.

Pressing "Cancel" results in this message: "Sending of the message failed. Unable to authenticate to Outgoing server (SMTP) . Please check the password and verify the 'Authentication method' in 'Account Settings | Outgoing server (SMTP)'."

Additionally I've reset my MS password several times. And MS seemingly is uninterested in having TBird/me login to my office365 account. I just get this response repeated over and over:

"Login to server smtp.office365.com with username [my.email.address] failed." With three button choices: "Retry" "Enter New Password" "Cancel".

I've spent a LOT of time over the past few days and over the weekend looking for a solution, but nothing so far has worked and I'm at my wit's end!!

Any help that you folks far more experienced with TBird can give me to get things working again would be GREATLY appreciated.

Best regards...

Tony
